## The big five

Teamwork can be broken down into five components

- Team leadership. Directs and coordinates
- Performance monitoring.
- Backup behaviour. Anticipate the other team member need, shift workloads where required
- Adaptability. Adjust strategy based on information gathered from backup behaviour and reallocate team resources.
- Team orientation. Prioritise team goals over individual members' goals.

Supporting mechanism

- Shared mental modes. Mental map of how tasks should be done
- Closed-loop communication. Exchange information between members
- Mutual trust. Belief team members will perform their roles.

![The ‘big five’ for team effectiveness model. The factors are represented by ovals while supporting mechanisms are shown as squares.](data:image/png;base64,)

Read the [Salas, Sims, and Burke (2005)](https://app.qa.com/download/dam/0b718081-5aa5-437c-8aca-2285889c6f95) paper and complete the following:

1. Think of a team project that you have recently taken part in.
2. Identify extent to which the Salas, Sims, and Burke core components and supporting mechanisms were evident in the project.
3. Would you add any core components or supporting mechanisms to their model to better represent data science projects?

Make notes on your thoughts for future reference.

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/activity-the-salas-sims-and-burke-core-components/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/activity-the-salas-sims-and-burke-core-components/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp)>

## Data science project teams

Table 1: Data science roles and expertise – adapted from Saltz (2024) 

|   |   |   |   |
|---|---|---|---|
||Level of expertise|||
|Role|Domain expertise|Technical knowledge|Quantitative skills|
|Data scientist|Medium|Medium|High|
|Data engineer|Low|High|Medium|
|Data science architect|Low|High|Low|
|Data science developer|Low|High|Medium|
|Product owner|Medium|Low|Low|
|Data/business analyst|Medium|Medium|Medium|
|Process master|Low|Low|Low|
|Subject matter expert|High|Low|Low|

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/data-science-project-teams/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/data-science-project-teams/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp)>

[https://www.datascience-pm.com/team-roles/](https://www.datascience-pm.com/team-roles/)

1. Which of the eight key data science team roles are you currently in?
2. What other data roles do you have?
3. What roles make up your data project teams?

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/activity-reflect-on-the-current-roles-in-your-team/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/activity-reflect-on-the-current-roles-in-your-team/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp)>

Data scientists - Me and others

System architect to manage platform

SME tax experts

Data analysts who use the data, provide feedback

## Building a data science capability

- The data science team. Concentrates on machine learning algorithms and discovering models
- The data engineering team. This team creates data pipelines and manages data storage and organisation at scale.
- The operation team. Looks after the infrastructure and makes sure everything keeps running.

Problems

- No high-ranking advocate for the data science team.
- Competing data science teams
- Shoehorning data science into software development management practices
- Insufficient buy-in from stakeholders
- Data science team isolated from business decisions
- Data science team has insufficient business understanding

After reading about the common pitfalls as identified by [Wood (2021)](https://fastdatascience.com/data-science-project-management/building-a-data-science-team/), answer the following questions:

1. Has the data capability at your organisation faced any of the issues as identified in the pitfalls by Wood (2021)?
2. Are there any other challenges that you face?
3. How can the challenges be overcome and is there anything you can do in your role to overcome the challenges?

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/activity-identify-your-challenges-as-an-ai-data-specialist-professional/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/activity-identify-your-challenges-as-an-ai-data-specialist-professional/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp)>

Hire a data engineer over a data scientist first. They can set everything up and have things based on best practice. A data scientist might not have the background on how things should be set up and might be frustrated by the limited data access.

Operations. Someone to take control of the day-today running of the models, quality control, ensuring servers run and load balancing.

## Importance of communication as an AI data specialist professional

This is highlighted in the following standards from your apprenticeship:

- K28: How to communicate concepts and present in a manner appropriate to diverse audiences, adapting communication techniques accordingly.
- S4: Communicate concepts and present in a manner appropriate to diverse audiences, adapting communication techniques accordingly.
- S27: Analyse information, frame questions, and conduct discussions with subject matter experts and assess existing data to scope new AI and data science requirements.
- B6: Is comfortable and confident interacting with people from technical and non-technical backgrounds. Presents data and conclusions in a truthful and appropriate manner.
- B2: Reliable, objective, and capable of independent and team working.

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/importance-of-communication-as-an-ai-data-specialist-professional/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/importance-of-communication-as-an-ai-data-specialist-professional/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp)>

1. At work, do you need to discuss AI concepts with diverse audiences? If so, how do you adapt your communication?
2. On a typical project, what type of work do you do independently and what type of work involves team working?
3. What sort of discussions do you have with subject matter experts and when?

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/activity-reflect-on-your-team-s-structure-and-communication/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/activity-reflect-on-your-team-s-structure-and-communication/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp)>

Customers, other data scientists and analysts who use that data. Give examples to some, others talk about f1 if they know what that means.

Get dev ops to ensure we have the platform.

Work with customers on what we can do

Work with other developers in Agile

References 

References are provided, as is the standard in academic writing, to show the provenance of the ideas expressed. You are not required to follow through and read every reference. 

- Anderson, J. (2020) Data Teams, Apress, O’Reilly, [Data Teams: A Unified Management Model for Successful Data-Focused Teams](https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/data-teams-a/9781484262283/)
- Salas, E., Sims, D.E. and Burke, C.S.(2005) ‘Is there a ‘big five’ in teamwork?’, Small group research, 36(5), available at: [salas_etal_2005_is_there_a_big_five_in_teamwork---copy.pdf (uio.no) (accessed 03-10-24)](https://www.uio.no/studier/emner/matnat/ifi/INF5181/h14/artikler-teamarbeid/salas_etal_2005_is_there_a_big_five_in_teamwork---copy.pdf) 
- Saltz, J. (2024) ‘8 Key Data Science Team Roles’, Data Science Process Alliance, March 27, available at: 8 Key Data Science Team Roles ([datascience-pm.com](https://datascience-pm.com/)), (accessed 03-10-2024) 
- Wood, T. (2021) ‘Building a data science team’, Fast Data Science, available at: [Building a data science team | Fast Data Science](https://fastdatascience.com/data-science-project-management/building-a-data-science-team/), (accessed 03-10-2024) 

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/further-reading-and-references-1730910579508/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/theme-8-team-working-1698/further-reading-and-references-1730910579508/?context_id=15255&context_resource=lp)>
